clean_body: Some of our first chances to fight back for our democracy: | Peter, the truth is there is no “offseason” in our line of work. There is no time off in defending our freedoms. So as we continue to analyze this year’s election results, we’re getting ready for some of our first opportunities to fight back for our democracy in 2025. Here’s what’s on the docket: | 🗓️ JANUARY 🗓️ Virginia will hold a special election that will decide control of the state senate AND the house of delegates. Currently, Democrats hold 21 seats and Republicans have 19 seats. If the GOP flips this seat, the Republican lieutenant governor would be the tie-breaking vote, giving them the majority. If Democrats win both seats, we will be able to fend off a Republican trifecta. Michigan will hold a special election that will set the balance of power in the state senate, as Democrats currently hold 20 seats and Republicans with 18 seats. If the GOP is able to turn this seat red, it would leave Democratic Lt. Gov. Garlin Gilchrist as the tie-breaking vote. 🗓️ APRIL 🗓️ Wisconsin will hold an election for a 10-year term on the state supreme court. While justices are elected as nonpartisan, the current breakdown of the court has four liberals and three conservatives. If conservatives are able to win this election, they will flip control of the court. 🗓️ NOVEMBER 🗓️ Virginia and New Jersey will hold statewide elections for governor, lieutenant governor, and the house of delegates. Pennsylvania will hold an election for three state supreme court seats. Currently, Democrats have a 5-2 partisan advantage on the court. But because three Democrats are up for reelection, losing just two of these elections will flip the makeup of the court. And sometime in 2025, Ohio will start the process of redrawing its congressional map. The new map will need to be completed by early 2026. Peter, I know that was a lot — but it’s critically important you understand what’s at stake over this next year, and why your support today matters so much. In 2025 and beyond, we’ll keep pushing to elect candidates who will defend our democracy, and that work has never been more critical. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thank you so much, Claire Claire Low Director of Political and Campaigns National Democratic Redistricting Committee = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, Suite #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States ** if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

name: Patti Reilly
subject: My police officer son is going to prison for doing his job
body:
Never in my wildest imagination could I have predicted that my son would be wrongfully convicted of murder – murder of a criminal my son didn’t even touch.
Dear Peter,

My son, Washington Metro Police Officer Terence Sutton, was just sentenced to 5 1⁄2 years in prison for doing the job he was trained to do.

And though you and I have never met, I’m praying you’ll help me save him.

Here are some photos of Terence as a little boy.

[](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_e=hm0b4gBW6ge_AGwY9M7xt7BhzRMTxvBEUdjtATzCUKyYJCoveGssqnv-vgOlvHLBYwoMiJGELUVlJQX6P0zP5oCwdBnsAeFxDbhpi74W0x8rDrYN17W8FpuEgLGPDJ0__FbgtBsFux0qFOGkjrNobHgcAjbNkx0NTjkUIDvIHLPjP0SnR9oWrNmO2S3pp_7nwlrkYqQ8xHggMAv9OhLr7pjdq9vBtf2fyCGdD46OQ3nb0DsBkFlrwS3bmOy-p7uV8Jzx2M8cw7naaUevE1z6nA5QqgdGWKW2shbYWr-klg0qEE6qPqfVbZHfvcw7un61-aN3wqD1PXrPNlf6UAPkRVO-CBqu6RqsNbOynKDmSRk7qepXd5usMKvEAtGs_QKvHhXwKqzn-QrbL3OLA1c8lg%3D%3D)
He always dreamed of following in his dad’s footsteps to be a police officer. I also included one of my favorites of us from the night he graduated from the Police Academy.

I was so proud that night.

Never in my wildest imagination could I have predicted that my son would be _wrongfully_ _convicted_ _of_ _murder_ **– murder of a criminal my son didn’t even touch.**

[**_I’m praying you will help me save my son_**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_e=hm0b4gBW6ge_AGwY9M7xt7BhzRMTxvBEUdjtATzCUKyYJCoveGssqnv-vgOlvHLBYwoMiJGELUVlJQX6P0zP5oCwdBnsAeFxDbhpi74W0x8rDrYN17W8FpuEgLGPDJ0__FbgtBsFux0qFOGkjrNobHgcAjbNkx0NTjkUIDvIHLPjP0SnR9oWrNmO2S3pp_7nwlrkYqQ8xHggMAv9OhLr7pjdq9vBtf2fyCGdD46OQ3nb0DsBkFlrwS3bmOy-p7uVhZKcJjw3xmBmxoWZFvByRMl4yYO1ZkSK_ZsUgrkjPV41gORmro8qRX2-8vTSDq7i-PdrteHZPBTWsyGmlAcXUoukg7HEAy6cWqJRx7Th4Np6ch6ZcJXL5OwLF-0KeYvD_gPk2CMowhK98RYsPpwCVg%3D%3D) – **but first, let me explain what happened.** Our nightmare started in October of 2020.

Terence was sitting in his patrol car when he received a report that a known gang member, Karon Hylton-Brown, was seen entering a rival gang’s territory.

Karon was a violent criminal with a rap sheet a mile long – including drugs, illegal gun possession, and assaults. Earlier that day, he was seen arguing with a rival gang member who owed him money. So Terence and other officers were concerned Karon was planning to get revenge.

When Terence saw Karon illegally driving a motorized scooter on the sidewalk without a helmet, he tried to pull him over to talk. Instead, Karon took off on the scooter.

Terence watched as Karon sped through traffic, weaving in and out of cars and blowing past red lights and stop signs.

My son knew Karon was putting others at risk, so for 3 minutes he slowly and carefully followed behind in his patrol car.

Then it happened.

Karon veered into an alleyway, drove out the other side without looking and slammed into a passing car.

After several days in the hospital, Karon died from his injuries. And here’s where the story takes a shocking turn...

Because Karon was black, and the incident happened just four months after George Floyd’s death, the anti-police mob went into frenzy.

They blamed Terence for the accident and smeared him as a racist.

There were protests and even riots. The mob threw rocks at officers, smashed store windows, and set fires.

Anti-police politicians piled on too. Without any evidence, they demanded my son be charged even though he did nothing to cause Karon’s death.

**The pressure campaign worked and prosecutors charged my son with Second-Degree Murder.**

**Terence was convicted in December 2022 and he was finally just sentenced – to 5 1⁄2 years in prison.**

Now you understand why I’m so heartbroken. The injustice is almost too much to bear.

My son was well-respected by the community he served. He trained many Rookies and set a good example for all of them. He was even awarded Officer of the Year!

But unless Terence’s appeal is successful, he’s going to prison – which is why I’m urgently turning to you for help today. The **Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund** (LELDF) has stepped forward to pay for Terence’s defense and is actively raising funds to pay for the appeal.

If you’ve ever heard of LELDF, you know they’re a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly-accused police officers like Terence for over 30 years. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent cops – and their families – at their lowest moments.

We expect it will cost at least $150,000 to appeal Terence’s conviction.

So I have to ask you one of the toughest questions I’ve ever asked anyone:

[**_Will you help my son Terence fight the unjust charges against him by donating to LELDF today?_**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_e=hm0b4gBW6ge_AGwY9M7xt7BhzRMTxvBEUdjtATzCUKyYJCoveGssqnv-vgOlvHLBYwoMiJGELUVlJQX6P0zP5oCwdBnsAeFxDbhpi74W0x8rDrYN17W8FpuEgLGPDJ0__FbgtBsFux0qFOGkjrNobHgcAjbNkx0NTjkUIDvIHLPjP0SnR9oWrNmO2S3pp_7nwlrkYqQ8xHggMAv9OhLr7pjdq9vBtf2fyCGdD46OQ3nb0DsBkFlrwS3bmOy-p7uVhgCDz_Vs1BwnT9gTzTPgvkzt_2RJpeDHh04WxTm8af2FQy54Xc7awYg30urmKjYiQyBglqTtNH7Y6vUe_SDGH255ynT9vpnqokQUob38IFRiRhcQejqMaGS4iWSPgZ1U2ASVPXvAFGqPATI457tKfA%3D%3D)

I’m not asking you to donate the entire $150,000 yourself – though it would be a huge weight off our shoulders if you are able to help that way.

[**_But your tax-deductible gift of $35, $50, $100, or more will help LELDF fully fund Terence’s appeal over the next 12 months._**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_e=hm0b4gBW6ge_AGwY9M7xt7BhzRMTxvBEUdjtATzCUKyYJCoveGssqnv-vgOlvHLBYwoMiJGELUVlJQX6P0zP5oCwdBnsAeFxDbhpi74W0x8rDrYN17W8FpuEgLGPDJ0__FbgtBsFux0qFOGkjrNobHgcAjbNkx0NTjkUIDvIHLPjP0SnR9oWrNmO2S3pp_7nwlrkYqQ8xHggMAv9OhLr7pjdq9vBtf2fyCGdD46OQ3nb0DsBkFlrwS3bmOy-p7uVKdqdzUrbD3wX87Y3p72kOIKH8wZoA0rgp7kxqSQSQ2y1ikJ6dR_lbWyNp0XqY6Kimk11qJerOFKSTmYaR9-1YEnzyi4AdO2l1STl5TEVFwcCCM7EOVFZFBcifGq6z9itTjjEbE6xy2YubcN5Dkd4nQ%3D%3D) If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn’t be so bold as to ask for your help.

But I already lost my husband. He was a police officer too, and he was so proud that Terence followed in his footsteps. I simply can’t bear the thought of having another family member ripped away from me.

[**_So if you are at all able, please follow this link to make a tax-deductible donation to the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund to help my Terence._**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_e=hm0b4gBW6ge_AGwY9M7xt7BhzRMTxvBEUdjtATzCUKyYJCoveGssqnv-vgOlvHLBYwoMiJGELUVlJQX6P0zP5oCwdBnsAeFxDbhpi74W0x8rDrYN17W8FpuEgLGPDJ0__FbgtBsFux0qFOGkjrNobHgcAjbNkx0NTjkUIDvIHLPjP0SnR9oWrNmO2S3pp_7nwlrkYqQ8xHggMAv9OhLr7pjdq9vBtf2fyCGdD46OQ3nb0DsBkFlrwS3bmOy-p7uVeqVZqdQTodw7RdKjloBNvYZSQnU6K1cGCjle2zRdPDsBYgVExCDbaI41eEtPTslcGkbyz-yFW9uUuHHqr_LgH8U2jRKDAk0XRF-nbKt_Fv_BIdWhJDDscnh5jLMOChvamwnJxcIG_gO8h-xzIwp_cA%3D%3D)

**Any amount – $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or more – will help.**

And because LELDF is a non-profit organization, your gift is completely tax-deductible. But whatever you can afford to give, I ask that you rush it back quickly.

Right now, Terence can only rely on the good nature and generosity of kind folks like you.

Thank you for taking the time to read my letter today.

And thank you in advance for helping LELDF support Terence and other wrongly accused officers.

Gratefully,
**Patti Reilly**

P.S. Don’t get me wrong, I’m thankful the judge granted Terence bond so he doesn’t have to report to prison just yet. Hopefully it never comes to that... but to get Terence’s conviction overturned, it’s going to cost at least $150,000 to fund his appeal. [**_Will you please help me save my son by making an urgent tax-deductible donation of $35, $50, $100 or more to LELDF today?_**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_e=hm0b4gBW6ge_AGwY9M7xt7BhzRMTxvBEUdjtATzCUKyYJCoveGssqnv-vgOlvHLBYwoMiJGELUVlJQX6P0zP5oCwdBnsAeFxDbhpi74W0x8rDrYN17W8FpuEgLGPDJ0__FbgtBsFux0qFOGkjrNobHgcAjbNkx0NTjkUIDvIHLPjP0SnR9oWrNmO2S3pp_7nwlrkYqQ8xHggMAv9OhLr7pjdq9vBtf2fyCGdD46OQ3nb0DsBkFlrwS3bmOy-p7uVlgQb5ZLWpQwBPrBZ-d7XiU0GF9LoR6wFEbcWUvJ_SYyX3aqKW3MLcfuSc3IY7VO0GRez5Xyvo8ynVhmhUDc4QVSvFWqzCYnpmooUCi5k5QnsAqqD0prIvRB9LYosxJKDRPjiRBA1p-9XsPmlvtltvQ%3D%3D) Thank you for standing with Terence during this nightmare.
--------

_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit**www.PoliceDefense.org**. And remember -Blue Lives Matter!_
_(C) Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | [www.PoliceDefense.org](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_e=hm0b4gBW6ge_AGwY9M7xt2TAHKRE5GPl9e1S8TBMJVJ5paxWZDWMAQPA5FCEqa8lqGzJ_XcC0KKuXio1_nj_0gUJG4bQ6I71tk71ZLanNyo0yPL9dlfZMw2dO9D4Ps8knZqVB4IjURHbnqJ4V3nKIQfPOkkNxSgSa3SjLuHP9BmF9P_mXBnQY5nY5VxXq5WpoNAcKCt8-FRqAs4r8O1mFQ%3D%3D)
2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303_

Click here to [Unsubscribe](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_s=b95d5301a70a4268bb8173dfd37ad468&_t=qCc0ZD1klaYG3Xg3uQCSnNOy_4ZAxMqlKBMynnM5cgTy7w0EJmZoZPRg1Yf0nwX19HFwYNTWQBSI_9Gw3yyAuIhkefUYxObqXBXJBszawhXDqZPJasz4E812UHanVIuQ)

name: Senate Republicans
subject: Our new Majority needs guidance. What issues matter MOST to you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
We took it ALL back! Now, we need to get to work. Please complete your Transition of Power survey TODAY to help our GOP leaders get to work on day one. | This won’t be a long email, Fellow American.

**_First_ - thank you for all your help with the campaign to take back the Senate. Without your help, we couldn’t have built this strong Republican majority.**

_Now, we need to ask for your help again, but we’re not asking for money today._

We need your help to let our GOP leaders know what issues matter MOST to the Republican voters who elected them to this new majority.

We hope to collect at least 5,000 of these SURVEY DOCUMENTS by**midnight tonight** to provide us with a representative sample of GOP voters to report to our GOP Majority what their FIRST PRIORITIES should be._ [**Please complete your Transition of Power survey today to help inform our leaders about their legislative battles with the far-left Democrats. >>>**](http://links.teamnrsc.co/u/click?_t=aff6d619248e4524b6f468bb9c83f15c&_m=7909e50ad7344be09b875d2818630085&_e=9lkEKDf7daISH5QranvyvBk0Q72nJtmTCtoKp1VSm11FNM1mLSGX9-OOzCFJiyF4g1SXtgoXsK3OEHjhJ8yl_ldNHfLcX2jVrS8cCQ63hPCKzvrYHI1EX_GQWa_Zf0zT4y3o1xGtHKmISFz0ZWRRAO-6rbX3UVkmoaxsjmxduG0MgvDs3sQ-wmhqbE_XoaCuV5Q9CGvDhJPngfas23xg6IHTqH4Nl-kwO2JLeh57vupknOqGcJF9g87TTS-5I0JCfZR_W-hdkjFEcJ6CPhIg5InD6souj1-LwD5PL3YzIy9u-sGQiwykc90ZXIdpQ2t0EzFGtVkFSB9U-AcU20CfWjgV_TBzmzQKAFc4jn5U7MZCHpuFhkzNilEUXrYpMc-OhMhOOMrWbAChdS2DpTZ6Aw6eiN0cF-Mdm4JRey5jSxkcRe4M74DQKOPf-vpmf6qeUtYRNJ06k9JUjZspo2Uc7w%3D%3D)

name: Michelle Steel [iPhone]
subject: We're not in the clear yet, Peter.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michelle Steel for Congress
body:
Sorry to reach out like this...

| |
---
| | | Peter, Election Day may be in the books, _but my race isn 't done yet._

_**[I'm neck and neck with my radical opponent, and ballots are still being counted as I send you this message.](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=1c379713daf74098941c1310e8d56443&_e=tddDo4MXhtKORDgmx0IYwWfobGTbUYHsF2VdU0dMBjIzuSRn376ruK1YWjF2us-T67hHApTbIwghEt0Ib1SPhux2Pdyt6eAkC2TJfQgG0QROyMm42PidMl7RcsuOvEC9beSexeJjxfup2u5n_LIch0fFeI_OhiyelokROcVKO61KR2RS6PiOuKrVs9qQGtIQsaIpu8-VVaxo5EsXXCmTbzjvM0qTsgEQtdBUuI1124xNTw26jwXZ-R1Dn-JSJqHI8PxfuAbZ67CJgC5jj21su7hjwCXmalTGR9rvacyZ58P3SnjhcYt-azj9iaP0OvI9A1XuL-VglsZxoGZe13pDntegWkSdU0JdRLF55n6rYOGi2hfjhbFfxjoZkyIfG-CuRXJ0-sFdI4g9FYJ8PEwRsAw3igdRk8LJc0n42IuOLU9vWyAJ6HXuNmysReXJ_FJknMGB6HZ8Fcu7S0ES2N1f_A%3D%3D "I’m neck and neck with my radical opponent, and ballots are still being counted as I send you this message.")**_

If we're going to pull this out and keep my seat, then I'm going to need all hands on deck.

I spent every dime I had just to get through Election Day, and now I'm running on fumes. I NEED your urgent support to help me keep this fight going before the ballots are all counted.

_**[Please, can I count on you, Peter?](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=1c379713daf74098941c1310e8d56443&_e=tddDo4MXhtKORDgmx0IYwWfobGTbUYHsF2VdU0dMBjIzuSRn376ruK1YWjF2us-T67hHApTbIwghEt0Ib1SPhux2Pdyt6eAkC2TJfQgG0QROyMm42PidMl7RcsuOvEC9beSexeJjxfup2u5n_LIch0fFeI_OhiyelokROcVKO61KR2RS6PiOuKrVs9qQGtIQsaIpu8-VVaxo5EsXXCmTbzjvM0qTsgEQtdBUuI1124xNTw26jwXZ-R1Dn-JSJqHI8PxfuAbZ67CJgC5jj21su7hjwCXmalTGR9rvacyZ58O_hVqnvpYI1oTWg7WcxWW6WO1QKPlVM-aFJWCnfI_U7SyL9hdtZaOXKya7nxt117Bt1HAIXW88mYsMIb3mgaNFd4uW54zWFLkh8XhBgxFMx4VwaEcjxHItp3d7B5r1HNdrClQo6DM92OV5vmb5CgN3u64RxDaCNvkdCtWvPArKrA%3D%3D "Please, can I count on you, Friend?")**_

Thank you,

Michelle Steel
_Sent from my iPhone_

subject: Kernel of Truth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
|
---
Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. _[If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520421/513695622/1416943042?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWE4NmMyMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rvPdd7sF4UcE6sPQ3JPkU9J2oAa2Zim-r3czJbEyVbs=&emci=178f8e4a-1ba9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=daa86c21-21a9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535)_ ****
_Election Shows Voters in Red States Support Paid Family Leave, Reproductive Freedom_ **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520422/513695623/-1621348527?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWE4NmMyMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rvPdd7sF4UcE6sPQ3JPkU9J2oAa2Zim-r3czJbEyVbs=&emci=178f8e4a-1ba9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=daa86c21-21a9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535)**
[ Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520423/513695624/-1621348527?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWE4NmMyMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rvPdd7sF4UcE6sPQ3JPkU9J2oAa2Zim-r3czJbEyVbs=&emci=178f8e4a-1ba9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=daa86c21-21a9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535) Indiana lacks a citizen-led ballot initiative system that many conservative states have in place, keeping Hoosiers from weighing in directly on important issues such as paid family leave, the minimum wage, reproductive rights, cannabis reform, and more. And based on the results from other red states across the country, if given the chance, Hoosiers would vote for many of those policies. Paid family leave passed in Alaska, Nebraska, and Missouri. Reproductive rights passed in Arizona, Missouri, and Montana. This continues to show that Hoosiers are being left behind when it comes to having a direct say on these important issues. It’s time to give the power back to the people and let Hoosiers decide like Americans do in other states across the country. ****_Incoming LG Beckwith Threatens Funding of Public Schools in Tantrum_ **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520424/513695625/1554293180?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWE4NmMyMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rvPdd7sF4UcE6sPQ3JPkU9J2oAa2Zim-r3czJbEyVbs=&emci=178f8e4a-1ba9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=daa86c21-21a9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535)**[ Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520425/513695626/1554293180?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWE4NmMyMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rvPdd7sF4UcE6sPQ3JPkU9J2oAa2Zim-r3czJbEyVbs=&emci=178f8e4a-1ba9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=daa86c21-21a9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535) This week Micah Beckwith lost it when Westfield Schools asked him not to come speak to a group of students. Instead of working toward a solution for the students involved, Beckwith said Westfield Schools deserved to have their funding stripped away, and that he would go around the state looking for other schools to target. It is intimidating behavior from the state’s incoming LG on a large scale, as he threatens the education of thousands of students and the jobs of teachers across the state. Beckwith also went on the radio and posted the personal phone number of Westfield Schools’ superintendent. Instead of worrying about the actual roles of his job, Beckwith is already out of control and endangering the future of our state. ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520426/513695627/392519613?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWE4NmMyMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rvPdd7sF4UcE6sPQ3JPkU9J2oAa2Zim-r3czJbEyVbs=&emci=178f8e4a-1ba9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=daa86c21-21a9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535)**[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520427/513695628/392519613?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWE4NmMyMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rvPdd7sF4UcE6sPQ3JPkU9J2oAa2Zim-r3czJbEyVbs=&emci=178f8e4a-1ba9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=daa86c21-21a9-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535) Over 13,000 Hoosier families have been forced in recent months onto the Indiana Medicaid waitlist, forcing families to make the impossible decision between spending much of their savings or being forced to wait for the care they need. This week, a class action lawsuit was filed against the state by Medicaid recipients who are being forced to wait. This is all despite the state sitting on an over $2 billion state surplus. Instead of spending that “rainy-day” money, it continues to be hoarded away and kept from the Hoosier taxpayers and families that need it most. clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. ** Election Shows Voters in Red States Support Paid Family Leave, Reproductive Freedom Indiana lacks a citizen-led ballot initiative system that many conservative states have in place, keeping Hoosiers from weighing in directly on important issues such as paid family leave, the minimum wage, reproductive rights, cannabis reform, and more. And based on the results from other red states across the country, if given the chance, Hoosiers would vote for many of those policies. Paid family leave passed in Alaska, Nebraska, and Missouri. Reproductive rights passed in Arizona, Missouri, and Montana. This continues to show that Hoosiers are being left behind when it comes to having a direct say on these important issues. It’s time to give the power back to the people and let Hoosiers decide like Americans do in other states across the country. Incoming LG Beckwith Threatens Funding of Public Schools in Tantrum This week Micah Beckwith lost it when Westfield Schools asked him not to come speak to a group of students. Instead of working toward a solution for the students involved, Beckwith said Westfield Schools deserved to have their funding stripped away, and that he would go around the state looking for other schools to target. It is intimidating behavior from the state’s incoming LG on a large scale, as he threatens the education of thousands of students and the jobs of teachers across the state. Beckwith also went on the radio and posted the personal phone number of Westfield Schools’ superintendent. Instead of worrying about the actual roles of his job, Beckwith is already out of control and endangering the future of our state. Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. Over 13,000 Hoosier families have been forced in recent months onto the Indiana Medicaid waitlist, forcing families to make the impossible decision between spending much of their savings or being forced to wait for the care they need. This week, a class action lawsuit was filed against the state by Medicaid recipients who are being forced to wait. This is all despite the state sitting on an over $2 billion state surplus. Instead of spending that “rainy-day” money, it continues to be hoarded away and kept from the Hoosier taxpayers and families that need it most. It is time for a real solution that puts these families first. ** RSVP for IDP’s Holiday Party = Reserve your spot at our annual Holiday Party to hear from Democratic leaders from across Indiana. = Get Involved! Opportunities After the Election As we move past the 2024 Election, it is imperative that we unite together to create a better future for our state. One of the best ways to do that is to help us rebuild our party and grow in the coming months and years. Upcoming Candidate Trainings Join the National Democratic Training Committee on Thursday, December 5th from 1-2 pm ET for Recruiting and Supporting Candidates to Run for Office , to develop actionable strategies for finding great candidates to run in your community. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: Dr. Ben Carson
subject: Preserving and strengthening America's families
body:
My name is Dr. Ben Carson, and along with my wife, Candy, I have spent years contemplating the challenges facing American families today. | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_s=b6e679a1b27442c19483fc03712d843f&_t=dQw8Ajwbz4pqvj9OiUN0d73lobUiAeWomJzBWbcIxkIOBbbx1AohKW83Ab3ZbNh2Jhh17YVtxWLAPBnG89J17wuML6O7LG0hAF_DAIMaZMnMc6rPWH_Id31SFg010Nci)_
---
---
| | | [](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=b6e679a1b27442c19483fc03712d843f&_e=nQwzmuBGbisc8ZLUn69RI1j7B8Yhe9eTOH9ql_re5ePeL36q3hq_Xz_4MriRT_ahub4-d4W52NmP6BP8Eox1asGzVwKivdiLAfF7PAAqngU8xWJyZc4K8I7hLeTITT6utyC7_cBjhIUawjFaPNZvmzE1mOqYsPOS6gYfaZANehwQAIMWRW7z2t4QDTiPlwMiO-8nkep6gqRRikIIQnWfOKpDwce2B_x2jNvjsihj3QMZ0g_nYrn2CqyUg_XXfAXpKDtom-z9cni95oW86VfLXOx0XvJjmB-jPYF3mUr9yFWG9rx5W_DFEvnKEAzU0PduumfcTiAUf10rIi5DAgYu7z0Xl4QXnKTPVBuYlJ-t02y0R2Ri7pXYyMq53aVvasxG4gmNfJTcrDJ7zh3n08WmZHfylNizSy3pE7Jiipx6P_c%3D)
---
| Dear Fellow Patriot,

My name is Dr. Ben Carson, and along with my wife, Candy, I have spent years contemplating the challenges facing American families today.

Our new book, _The Perilous Fight_ , addresses these issues head-on, offering practical insights and solutions to restore and strengthen the family unit.

Why is this important? Because the family's pivotal role in our society is under threat, and without addressing these challenges, our foundational values may be lost. [](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=b6e679a1b27442c19483fc03712d843f&_e=nQwzmuBGbisc8ZLUn69RI1j7B8Yhe9eTOH9ql_re5ePeL36q3hq_Xz_4MriRT_ahub4-d4W52NmP6BP8Eox1asGzVwKivdiLAfF7PAAqngU8xWJyZc4K8I7hLeTITT6utyC7_cBjhIUawjFaPNZvmzE1mOqYsPOS6gYfaZANehwQAIMWRW7z2t4QDTiPlwMiO-8nkep6gqRRikIIQnWfOKpDwce2B_x2jNvjsihj3QMZ0g_nYrn2CqyUg_XXfAXpKDtom-z9cni95oW86VfLXJ3sSn0hN60nlv-TUvkFB3RT_2h5EfUmzxrd2-yeu3ht363ICXEusSvNHT0bGr7jVhRr6c8xl62ORjkLYtmPV78oiONDS0Z3-x91Eh-3qu3CCJLqjoqH0DLBkscAG3qSC5-r4LMfVNFdNSVC71NP5vY%3D) In _The Perilous Fight_ , we dive deep into the social and economic pressures that families face, dissect the erosion of traditional values, and present actionable steps for rebuilding familial bonds. This book isn't just a series of observations; it's a roadmap to a healthier, more stable society through strong family units.

**[**As someone committed to the promotion of truth and values, I am offering to send you my new book _"The Perilous Fight" with_ a gift of $45 or more.**](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=b6e679a1b27442c19483fc03712d843f&_e=nQwzmuBGbisc8ZLUn69RI1j7B8Yhe9eTOH9ql_re5ePeL36q3hq_Xz_4MriRT_ahub4-d4W52NmP6BP8Eox1asGzVwKivdiLAfF7PAAqngU8xWJyZc4K8I7hLeTITT6utyC7_cBjhIUawjFaPNZvmzE1mOqYsPOS6gYfaZANehwQAIMWRW7z2t4QDTiPlwMiO-8nkep6gqRRikIIQnWfOKpDwce2B_x2jNvjsihj3QMZ0g_nYrn2CqyUg_XXfAXpKDtom-z9cni95oW86VfLXDCR6QsmpDWLNFimJ2l7L6Wcx7DGg-bGQGP58mgoG75a9-AEWWrw-s54hVmaB6toVASdoHpbjGbTPeKjJZOFDiYMfLdg9UQD0SvDAXXXg6GzLA9MDpu-rbXnN_htHr0cWzOWGC8DKyPWlkbuAzyThAs%3D)** _The Perilous Fight _with allow you to:
* Understand the current challenges American families face;

* Discover actionable steps to strengthen your family and community; and

* Be part of a broader effort to uphold traditional values and promote societal stability

The American Cornerstone Institute is devoted to advancing faith, liberty, community, and life. By making a gift, you are taking a crucial step towards healing our nation, starting at its very heart--the family.

[Click here to make your gift of $45 or more and receive your own copy of _The Perilous Fight_ now and join us in the mission to strengthen American families.](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=b6e679a1b27442c19483fc03712d843f&_e=nQwzmuBGbisc8ZLUn69RI1j7B8Yhe9eTOH9ql_re5ePeL36q3hq_Xz_4MriRT_ahub4-d4W52NmP6BP8Eox1asGzVwKivdiLAfF7PAAqngU8xWJyZc4K8I7hLeTITT6utyC7_cBjhIUawjFaPNZvmzE1mOqYsPOS6gYfaZANehwQAIMWRW7z2t4QDTiPlwMiO-8nkep6gqRRikIIQnWfOKpDwce2B_x2jNvjsihj3QMZ0g_nYrn2CqyUg_XXfAXpKDtom-z9cni95oW86VfLXK2rJlDc7FFo93YtTVk2-a0m0Zj0OHE0YvL_4UcvNJNnlgKY-YGq3MBVi8V4o7kJQSVhEWjjo-fJZ4CfF7CfH0UMfdy2d3vPNPOi1O8AkvIbpwRdzN6LsJp3h8WQpSOat0zbV0QKDVz8rx96QKWi1yA%3D)

Sincerely,

Dr. Ben Carson
Founder and Chairman
American Cornerstone Institute

name: Lucas Kunce via Substack
subject: What were Trump voters like on the campaign trail?
body:
Having been in every corner of the state and talked to thousands of people, I can confidently say it's not the same with everyone.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.JNvEJrTa00II9iYs7BeHPrhM6Jc8atGHBAyqKLjM2fU?) for more

[](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wL3doYXQtd2VyZS10cnVtcC12b3RlcnMtbGlrZS1vbi10aGU_.Q5KEt2nDfZAa9cAQWbJwlUez-DfWQBkgREqUNhTMVaA?)
---
# [What were Trump voters like on the campaign trail?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=152032910&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMDMyOTEwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzIzMTQ3ODIsImV4cCI6MTczNDkwNjc4MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XipYEpb5QtqJZdWgIQvH3SpT3driqVe6To4S3KS-RAA)
### Having been in every corner of the state and talked to thousands of people, I can confidently say it's not the same with everyone.
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucaskunce)
---
| Nov 22
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucaskunce)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=152032910&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMDMyOTEwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczMjMxNDc4MiwiZXhwIjoxNzM0OTA2NzgyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TKNFDzZ8kbjEsLOcaxWtyDc4ZmZFAgshTRh7-LGlmWM&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=152032910&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMDMyOTEwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzIzMTQ3ODIsImV4cCI6MTczNDkwNjc4MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XipYEpb5QtqJZdWgIQvH3SpT3driqVe6To4S3KS-RAA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=152032910&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUyMDMyOTEwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzIzMTQ3ODIsImV4cCI6MTczNDkwNjc4MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.XipYEpb5QtqJZdWgIQvH3SpT3driqVe6To4S3KS-RAA)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvbHVjYXNrdW5jZS9wL3doYXQtd2VyZS10cnVtcC12b3RlcnMtbGlrZS1vbi10aGU_.UST5oim_CdDJG-njBx6uXky2gLCRap-bc1iNeKyV3CY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/what-were-trump-voters-like-on-the?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

First, thank you to everyone for all the nice comments after my last message. To have your support is an incredible blessing and has inspired me to continue with this substack and start answering some of the questions you have had.
I'm working on a more in depth analysis of the campaign that we can all try to learn from (in the Marine Corps we call it an after action), but before that is done, I wanted to answer a question I have been getting from a lot of people who didn't vote for Trump:
What were Trump voters like on the campaign trail?
Having been in every corner of the state and talked to thousands of people, I can confidently say it's not the same with everyone.
A decent number said they were actually going to vote for me, and tens of thousands of them did -- my opponent received 100,000 fewer votes in Missouri than Trump did. Many told me and my team that they wish they could vote for me, but couldn't because of what the national Democratic Party meant to them. Here, in their own words, are some examples of what both of these kinds of voters had to say in emails, texts, and conversations on the trail.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f1762459-8059-4b78-91ff-78a2e5a0e47d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_A selection of emails and texts from voters_
While tens of thousands did switch over to vote for me and Donald Trump, many everyday working Missourians believe that Donald Trump will make their lives better and couldn't bring themselves to support anyone who wasn't a Republican because of it.
In the final weeks of the campaign I went to polling places all around the state and talked to people while they were waiting in line to vote. As you would expect, there were a lot of Trump voters out there. Here are a couple of examples of how they felt that I experienced over and over again.
The first guy I'll give an example of was a 60 or so year old white man. He told me that he had been working his whole life since he was a kid, that his business fixing appliances collapsed during COVID, that he couldn't afford groceries anymore, and that he wouldn't survive without Social Security in a few years. I believe that every single one of those things about his life was true.
He blamed Biden for destroying his business during COVID and for making groceries unaffordable and believed that Donald Trump was the only one who could fix the economy and save Social Security. And he just couldn't vote for me because he couldn't be sure I would support Trump's efforts to Make America Great Again.
On the Saturday before the election I was at an early voting place at a mall in St. Louis County. The line to vote wrapped almost entirely around the mall. As I said hello to people I saw two latino men around 30 years old quietly standing in line and not looking at me. I went up to say hi and they politely said hi back.
I told them who I was and that I wanted to meet people and ask for their consideration or see if they had any questions.
The older of the two, in pretty heavily accented English, said that he knew who I was, that he was sure I was a nice guy and that he liked me more than Hawley, but that there was no way he could vote for me. I asked him if there was anything I could do to change his mind.
He said he was sorry, but that he worked for a living and that Democrats had ruined the economy. That Trump was a businessman and therefore was the only one who could fix it, and that he just couldn't vote for me because I was a Democrat and Trump was a Republican. This wasn't the first time I had had a conversation like this with a latino immigrant.
The takeaway from these, thousands more encounters along the campaign trail, and more of our campaign's research is that many working people believe that they have found a political home -- and it is not with the Democratic Party. It's not clear that it's with the Republican Party necessarily either, but more with Trump's party -- we'll see if that impact sticks in future elections without Trump on the ballot.
An interesting thing that Trump has done in Missouri is that he brings out hundreds of thousands of infrequent Republican voters in the state who don't necessarily vote otherwise -- particularly in these last two presidential elections. Time will tell if this impact will continue without Trump.
With the new makeup of the Senate and the House, it seems like the most likely big result of the next four years is a massive tax cut for big corporations and the richest Americans. I hope I'm wrong and that everyday people's lives improve.
I'll work on getting out a more in depth analysis soon, but until then, post your questions in the comments or shoot me an email and I'll see what else I can answer.
Lucas

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/217d3951-68e0-4f51-af53-d777211da005?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

I'll work on getting out a more in depth analysis soon, but until then, post your questions in the comments or shoot me an email and I'll see what else I can answer. * **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas Supreme Court: Attorney General Ken Paxton need not testify in whistleblower case,** " Austin American Statesman's **Bayliss Wagner** -- "Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and three senior aides do not have to testify in a wrongful termination case filed by four whistleblowers against Paxton's office, the Texas Supreme Court ruled Friday.

The unanimous decision deals a crushing blow to the case brought by Paxton's former employees after their yearslong battle to compel his deposition.

Eight of the court's nine conservative justices -- all but Justice**Evan Young** , who recused himself -- found the depositions were unnecessary because the top lawyer agreed not to contest the plaintiffs' allegations, thus resolving any factual dispute that witnesses could shed light on.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/34746023-ee08-4c0b-8dba-266778510d9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"The only fact issue on which those witnesses are likely to provide information--OAG's liability under the Whistleblower Act--is now uncontested," the court wrote in [an unsigned order.](https://substack.com/redirect/afc70ec0-6b92-4d04-81f2-0315880fcdc6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Their decision partially overturns rulings from a trial court and the 3rd Court of Appeals [that found Paxton must testify.](https://substack.com/redirect/53edec3a-5c56-42f6-92ff-456b7e95c95b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

The state Supreme Court had rejected Paxton's previous attempt to kill the court-ordered deposition [in a divided ruling in January.](https://substack.com/redirect/19c25582-18d3-48f0-a474-c9bbafadfd08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

After Paxton said he would stop fighting the claims, the plaintiffs had argued that allowing Paxton to skirt his deposition would jeopardize a future Legislature-funded settlement and deter future state employees from reporting improper conduct to authorities.

"Knowing that a government defendant could cut short litigation without admitting the claims or allowing them to be developed, while continuing to publicly smear plaintiffs and curtailing any effective relief, would only chill those whom the law was meant to safeguard," the whistleblowers' attorneys wrote in the June 25 filing.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/34746023-ee08-4c0b-8dba-266778510d9e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
A previous, tentative $3.3 million settlement agreement between Paxton and the whistleblowers failed in 2023 after Paxton asked for a legislative appropriation to fund the settlement, which prompted House Speaker **Dade Phelan** , R-Beaumont, to tap the House Committee on General Investigating to look into the situation. After the House overwhelmingly voted to impeach Paxton on 20 charges, the state Senate acquitted him in a mostly party-line vote.

Paxton -- along with First Assistant Attorney General**Brent Webster** ; Paxton's chief of staff, **Lesley French Henneke** ; and senior adviser **Michelle Smith** -- [had been scheduled](https://substack.com/redirect/1997b70c-7f0b-4268-80b3-1316e506d149?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to be deposed in February prior to Paxton's current appeal to the Supreme Court.

The lawsuit filed by the whistleblowers -- former employees **James Blake Brickman** , **J. Mark Penley** , **David Maxwell** , and **Ryan M. Vassar** -- in 2021 will continue with other forms of discovery." [AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/0e73be46-f5ec-43fc-82bf-3fe2d12788f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

The unanimous decision deals a crushing blow to the case brought by Paxton's former employees after their yearslong battle to compel his deposition. Eight of the court's nine conservative justices -- all but JusticeEvan Young , who recused himself -- found the depositions were unnecessary because the top lawyer agreed not to contest the plaintiffs' allegations, thus resolving any factual dispute that witnesses could shed light on. "The only fact issue on which those witnesses are likely to provide information--OAG's liability under the Whistleblower Act--is now uncontested," the court wrote in Their decision partially overturns rulings from a trial court and the 3rd Court of Appeals The state Supreme Court had rejected Paxton's previous attempt to kill the court-ordered deposition After Paxton said he would stop fighting the claims, the plaintiffs had argued that allowing Paxton to skirt his deposition would jeopardize a future Legislature-funded settlement and deter future state employees from reporting improper conduct to authorities. "Knowing that a government defendant could cut short litigation without admitting the claims or allowing them to be developed, while continuing to publicly smear plaintiffs and curtailing any effective relief, would only chill those whom the law was meant to safeguard," the whistleblowers' attorneys wrote in the June 25 filing. A previous, tentative $3.3 million settlement agreement between Paxton and the whistleblowers failed in 2023 after Paxton asked for a legislative appropriation to fund the settlement, which prompted House Speaker Dade Phelan , R-Beaumont, to tap the House Committee on General Investigating to look into the situation. After the House overwhelmingly voted to impeach Paxton on 20 charges, the state Senate acquitted him in a mostly party-line vote. Paxton -- along with First Assistant Attorney GeneralBrent Webster ; Paxton's chief of staff, Lesley French Henneke ; and senior adviser Michelle Smith -- to be deposed in February prior to Paxton's current appeal to the Supreme Court. The lawsuit filed by the whistleblowers -- former employees James Blake Brickman , J. Mark Penley , David Maxwell , and Ryan M. Vassar -- in 2021 will continue with other forms of discovery." * **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas Supreme Court: Attorney General Ken Paxton need not testify in whistleblower case,** " Austin American Statesman's **Bayliss Wagner** -- "Texas Attorney General **Ken Paxton** and three senior aides do not have to testify in a wrongful termination case filed by four whistleblowers against Paxton's office, the Texas Supreme Court ruled Friday.

The unanimous decision deals a crushing blow to the case brought by Paxton's former employees after their yearslong battle to compel his deposition.

Eight of the court's nine conservative justices -- all but Justice**Evan Young** , who recused himself -- found the depositions were unnecessary because the top lawyer agreed not to contest the plaintiffs' allegations, thus resolving any factual dispute that witnesses could shed light on.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/00e146c5-09e0-4112-a828-412135b756ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"The only fact issue on which those witnesses are likely to provide information--OAG's liability under the Whistleblower Act--is now uncontested," the court wrote in [an unsigned order.](https://substack.com/redirect/0b8c1bd1-0057-4a3f-bb66-605a0ccfac0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Their decision partially overturns rulings from a trial court and the 3rd Court of Appeals [that found Paxton must testify.](https://substack.com/redirect/1ba8ce37-7eac-4841-927b-5ece4c289b33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

The state Supreme Court had rejected Paxton's previous attempt to kill the court-ordered deposition [in a divided ruling in January.](https://substack.com/redirect/bf5294f0-c827-4849-a8f0-8c87b0e6642f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

After Paxton said he would stop fighting the claims, the plaintiffs had argued that allowing Paxton to skirt his deposition would jeopardize a future Legislature-funded settlement and deter future state employees from reporting improper conduct to authorities.

"Knowing that a government defendant could cut short litigation without admitting the claims or allowing them to be developed, while continuing to publicly smear plaintiffs and curtailing any effective relief, would only chill those whom the law was meant to safeguard," the whistleblowers' attorneys wrote in the June 25 filing.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/00e146c5-09e0-4112-a828-412135b756ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
A previous, tentative $3.3 million settlement agreement between Paxton and the whistleblowers failed in 2023 after Paxton asked for a legislative appropriation to fund the settlement, which prompted House Speaker **Dade Phelan** , R-Beaumont, to tap the House Committee on General Investigating to look into the situation. After the House overwhelmingly voted to impeach Paxton on 20 charges, the state Senate acquitted him in a mostly party-line vote.

Paxton -- along with First Assistant Attorney General**Brent Webster** ; Paxton's chief of staff, **Lesley French Henneke** ; and senior adviser **Michelle Smith** -- [had been scheduled](https://substack.com/redirect/f474efb5-9539-4180-a0d4-af3691344cb4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to be deposed in February prior to Paxton's current appeal to the Supreme Court.

The lawsuit filed by the whistleblowers -- former employees **James Blake Brickman** , **J. Mark Penley** , **David Maxwell** , and **Ryan M. Vassar** -- in 2021 will continue with other forms of discovery." ($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) TOP NEWS "Texas Supreme Court: Attorney General Ken Paxton need not testify in whistleblower case, " Austin American Statesman's Bayliss Wagner -- "Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton and three senior aides do not have to testify in a wrongful termination case filed by four whistleblowers against Paxton's office, the Texas Supreme Court ruled Friday. The unanimous decision deals a crushing blow to the case brought by Paxton's former employees after their yearslong battle to compel his deposition. Eight of the court's nine conservative justices -- all but JusticeEvan Young , who recused himself -- found the depositions were unnecessary because the top lawyer agreed not to contest the plaintiffs' allegations, thus resolving any factual dispute that witnesses could shed light on. "The only fact issue on which those witnesses are likely to provide information--OAG's liability under the Whistleblower Act--is now uncontested," the court wrote in Their decision partially overturns rulings from a trial court and the 3rd Court of Appeals The state Supreme Court had rejected Paxton's previous attempt to kill the court-ordered deposition After Paxton said he would stop fighting the claims, the plaintiffs had argued that allowing Paxton to skirt his deposition would jeopardize a future Legislature-funded settlement and deter future state employees from reporting improper conduct to authorities. "Knowing that a government defendant could cut short litigation without admitting the claims or allowing them to be developed, while continuing to publicly smear plaintiffs and curtailing any effective relief, would only chill those whom the law was meant to safeguard," the whistleblowers' attorneys wrote in the June 25 filing. A previous, tentative $3.3 million settlement agreement between Paxton and the whistleblowers failed in 2023 after Paxton asked for a legislative appropriation to fund the settlement, which prompted House Speaker Dade Phelan , R-Beaumont, to tap the House Committee on General Investigating to look into the situation. After the House overwhelmingly voted to impeach Paxton on 20 charges, the state Senate acquitted him in a mostly party-line vote. Paxton -- along with First Assistant Attorney GeneralBrent Webster ; Paxton's chief of staff, Lesley French Henneke ; and senior adviser Michelle Smith -- to be deposed in February prior to Paxton's current appeal to the Supreme Court. The lawsuit filed by the whistleblowers -- former employees James Blake Brickman , J. Mark Penley , David Maxwell , and Ryan M. Vassar -- in 2021 will continue with other forms of discovery." ($) FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. name: Sarah Curmi, EMILYs List
subject: We owe it to voters.
body:
Will you help this pro-choice team see this recount process through? |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/97520661/513696616/126959580?refcode=WTEFHW55_20241122_RiggsRecount_PL&sourceid=1055454&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODQyMTdlMS0yMWE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ki4fLTTWg_u4vKtZP7xxgMFMydtOKEFQktNQyXcNMlI=) Hi Peter, it's Sarah, VP of State and Local Campaigns at EMILYs List, writing to you about a big race in North Carolina.

**We're thrilled that Justice Allison Riggs won her race for the North Carolina Supreme Court!**

Our courts directly impact the strength of our democracy, our access to reproductive health care, and our children's access to public education, and we're so grateful to the North Carolinians who made this victory possible.

**Justice Riggs' race isn't quite over yet, though. We owe it to voters to ensure that every vote is counted — which is why a recount is currently underway in North Carolina. **

Recounts are a normal electoral process that allow for transparency and promote public confidence in our electoral systems. Based on what we're seeing so far, we expect that this recount will continue to proceed smoothly, confirm Justice Riggs' win, and affirm the great work done by North Carolina state election officials.

But her opponent has already filed over 300 protests to try and throw out 60,000 ballots. Peter, we won't be fooled by this blatant attempt to disenfranchise voters.

We're thrilled that Justice Allison Riggs won her race for the North Carolina Supreme Court! Our courts directly impact the strength of our democracy, our access to reproductive health care, and our children's access to public education, and we're so grateful to the North Carolinians who made this victory possible. Justice Riggs' race isn't quite over yet, though. We owe it to voters to ensure that every vote is counted — which is why a recount is currently underway in North Carolina. name: Leaders We Deserve
subject: Hope isn't a strategy. This is.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Together, we're writing the next story of progressive leadership
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20241122&t=1&akid=1465%2E587540%2EEDRUKs)
We need your help to shape the future of our progressive movement, Peter.
We're launching a critical survey to map out our strategy for the next two years, and your insights are essential. This isn't just another questionnaire – it's a blueprint for how we build progressive power from the ground up.
**[Take our Movement-Defining Survey »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/survey/lwd-em-redefine-movement-20241122?source=em20241122-1465&t=2&akid=1465%2E587540%2EEDRUKs)**
This year, we've witnessed the transformative power of investing in young, bold leadership – proving that change isn't just possible, it's happening right now.
🏳️⚧️ **Sarah McBride** became the first openly transgender member of Congress in Delaware, shattering historic barriers and proving that authentic representation matters.
📚 **Bryce Berry** made history as the youngest active public school teacher elected to the Georgia State Legislature, bringing firsthand educator insights directly into policy-making.
🏛️ **Dante Pittman** strategically broke the Republican supermajority in North Carolina, creating critical space for progressive governance.
🤝 **Molly Cook** continued her groundbreaking work in the Texas Senate, demonstrating that young leaders can create lasting change and inspire a new generation of political engagement.
🌟 **Oscar De Los Santos** brought fresh energy to the Arizona House, representing a new generation of political leadership with bold ideas and authentic community connections.
🔥 **Christine Cockley** challenges political norms as one of the youngest voices in the Ohio House, proving age is no barrier to impact.
These aren't just wins. They're a movement. A promise. A declaration that young people are not just the future of politics — we are the present.
**[Share your vision here »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/survey/lwd-em-redefine-movement-20241122?source=em20241122-1465&t=3&akid=1465%2E587540%2EEDRUKs)**
Help us build on this momentum. Your perspective will shape our next steps.
In hope and solidarity,
Leaders We Deserve
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20241122&t=4&refcode2=1465_587540_EDRUKs&akid=1465%2E587540%2EEDRUKs)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=5&akid=1465%2E587540%2EEDRUKs)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=6&akid=1465%2E587540%2EEDRUKs)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)

subject: They’re trying to start World War III before Trump takes office
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROYCE WHITE FOR SENATE
body:
It's a final screw you to the American People.
| [ ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=0cf7a5f61c0544d8a82cbcf2f84c4274&_e=uXOfbFt9m2nhjp9-YapkRSRZS9I2g9bc6RQz6D3Nt4W-Zc8BBceR9PY5IWGNatJvSFpwChe6zTRthgkKj7qlp5HdEtJBiGZ8tjyUTnRfdl03iCOoN9iIEakuT4PR0g_ovEAqWG1WGVVdQkcGoiLKPDAsuP12uZe5p9boyoJjoJCsC2csEC53UrEtKUS_XGrPzmtvmh_hOHySDfAgf3mpgk2yV58tV50SM5DrSDYu08OEN2eeufzkylIvykSjwi_i7RA45GbypPypnhM4HRHus_3ZGS9Sk-2OYpKN_lVw81pNXXpM3GTPZp2kFPGhdy-WMaL91VtI_f_-JSZ50IW09w%3D%3D)
---

Peter,

**I am DEEPLY CONCERNED! Unless you've been living under a rock, I'm sure you've seen the wall-to-wall news coverage of how Biden and his handlers are well on their way to starting World War 3.**

And it's in high gear now because Biden officially signed off on Ukraine using American-made missiles against Russia.

_**A ESCALATION that DIRECTLY DENIES THE WILL OF THE AMERICAN PEOPLE -- even the liberal news outlets!

When Trump won in a LANDSLIDE, it was a clear mandate from Americans that we need to STOP ESCALATING the war between Ukraine and Russia.**_

This is the globalists' last chance before the inauguration... A long shot, but worth it in their twisted minds if they can get it done.

It is Biden's nasty parting gift to the US -- the American People -- from him and his globalist regime. Joe Biden, you're a jerk. No Unity!

| [ **PREVENT WORLD WAR III** ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=0cf7a5f61c0544d8a82cbcf2f84c4274&_e=uXOfbFt9m2nhjp9-YapkRSRZS9I2g9bc6RQz6D3Nt4W-Zc8BBceR9PY5IWGNatJvSFpwChe6zTRthgkKj7qlp5HdEtJBiGZ8tjyUTnRfdl03iCOoN9iIEakuT4PR0g_ovEAqWG1WGVVdQkcGoiLKPDAsuP12uZe5p9boyoJjoJCsC2csEC53UrEtKUS_XGrPVPdRtgWO2qaAot0qUDW-M_79xaYHF8gaR2gSk7DM09tluuoKUn-bZ38GmjG9hlzvqubF29o-n5a-CizvhmG0I6yCwouHhymTb-2g9pPS4bG5qoa97K6wc2y9epXf5Y7HOL6emVjqBO-5ayDCsYPJyg%3D%3D)
---

The Radical Left will never stop, regardless of how clearly Americans shout our America-First message. That's why I need your help today. I need your help in easing our concerns about escalations with Russia before President Trump has the opportunity to resolve the conflict through peace and strength.

[**I 'm humbly asking you to send a donation of whatever you can afford to help me EXPOSE AND DEFEAT the Radical Left and the Neocon Puppets.**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=0cf7a5f61c0544d8a82cbcf2f84c4274&_e=uXOfbFt9m2nhjp9-YapkRSRZS9I2g9bc6RQz6D3Nt4W-Zc8BBceR9PY5IWGNatJvSFpwChe6zTRthgkKj7qlp5HdEtJBiGZ8tjyUTnRfdl03iCOoN9iIEakuT4PR0g_ovEAqWG1WGVVdQkcGoiLKPDAsuP12uZe5p9boyoJjoJCsC2csEC53UrEtKUS_XGrPjBX93ROWH1O9lrRew5krezYdHJUGQV2mJbDr4nCbHTGuGYWCbZ6N0D4Y-sPj48undNME2_KhyHZ-VC1uz9qfPokdWcEShaxLvaBL3kJr28xl7EgJ3hzEOwSAu9E7Oi9QmyvOHDaDs0qla2H6udGkpQ%3D%3D)

Godspeed,
[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=0cf7a5f61c0544d8a82cbcf2f84c4274&_e=uXOfbFt9m2nhjp9-YapkRSRZS9I2g9bc6RQz6D3Nt4W-Zc8BBceR9PY5IWGNatJvSFpwChe6zTRthgkKj7qlp5HdEtJBiGZ8tjyUTnRfdl03iCOoN9iIEakuT4PR0g_ovEAqWG1WGVVdQkcGoiLKPDAsuP12uZe5p9boyoJjoJCsC2csEC53UrEtKUS_XGrPeRQUfdXuHj-TdkCCyDXV-5XWzrLGt0quhTuag8b96LTEfdWSJfaD6E7bNcZrJ7OvY6JrtYgjzaqpbTKwfpF8A4WiZaSbLAXwVMcKiiMVNuljTH0AgbTH91OHpi9nAwnFuhXkye8HacKw9RahZ_gLWQ%3D%3D)
Royce White

[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=0cf7a5f61c0544d8a82cbcf2f84c4274&_e=uXOfbFt9m2nhjp9-YapkRSRZS9I2g9bc6RQz6D3Nt4W-Zc8BBceR9PY5IWGNatJvSFpwChe6zTRthgkKj7qlp5HdEtJBiGZ8tjyUTnRfdl03iCOoN9iIEakuT4PR0g_ovEAqWG1WGVVdQkcGoiLKPDAsuP12uZe5p9boyoJjoJCsC2csEC53UrEtKUS_XGrP6OhnPr7TbvmVstKT00lXid2TwkXYsYDR-SrCwIuEgcVryWBeynQfv_zvpAK0KLNRKUoXzTKuQNR47PeKr8yJKHyF8yTKd-4tzf8anozYrqsXciOD5BZHKOwPSEORmr7MZ0j23xQ9B9ICl4XHsDUAyA%3D%3D)

Meet Royce White:

**Born and raised in the Twin Cities, Royce White was the son of a single mother and started playing sports at the age of 5 years old. Living in various neighborhoods growing up, much of his family remained in the Rondo community, where he spent much of his time and received schooling. White excelled at basketball and recieved a scholarship to play basketball at the University of Minnesota, and later transferred to Iowa State. He would go on to make Dean's List while receiving All Big-12 first-team honors and many other basketball awards.

_After being selected 16th overall by The Houston Rockets in the NBA draft, White began to voice concerns about the NBA 's collective bargaining agreement (CBA), specifically regarding mental health policy, which at the time did not exist. Eventually, his concerns became a public dispute between White and the NBA. This resulted in White demanding that the NBA, as a representative of the global corporate community, take up the responsibility of spearheading the mental health conversation - not just posturing through press statements, but through policy._

Royce then went on to win two championships in Canada before moving over to play Big 3 Basketball. White appeared at a game in a t-shirt that read "Free The Uyghurs." After a game-winning basket, White did a post-game interview on CBS, where he called for 2 Million Uyghurs to be freed and released from concentration camps. As the season went on, White would touch on taboo topics such as Gain of Function Research, The Federal Reserve, Julian Assange, Edward Snowden, Larry Hoover, Jeff Fort, Muammar Gaddafi, The Great Reset, Ivermectin, Me Too, etc.

Now, Royce White is working tirelessly to FLIP Minnesota Red and give President Trump the MAGA reinforcements he needs. Can you help him by clicking the button below?

subject: Sneak peek: New office!

body:
Since 1908, new members of the House of Representatives have chosen their offices in order via a lottery system.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520908/513697232/-1719677217?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2024-11-22_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTI0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWFkYjk3NS0yNGE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hIjWgpe-8r4xd9t9Dw8YAgzSOg7kSq8gc50s56BZpM8=)
---
| Since 1908, new members of the House of Representatives have chosen their offices in order via a lottery system. Prior to this, members simply worked from their desks on the floor. Yesterday, our exhausted class drew numbers (by random number generator) to determine our order. Through some sort of luck...I drew number 21 of 57 freshmen.

My team and I then double-checked our handy, color-coded list and revisited a number of open offices. There are so many factors to consider: square footage, stairs versus elevator, distance to the Capitol and potential committee assignments, space to welcome visitors and take meetings. Every single office is unique and comes with its own set of quirks, like a closet that houses a sink or the office that once belonged to LBJ. Ultimately, we chose Longworth House Office Building, Suite 1213.

I’m grateful to the current tenant’s staff who graciously let us visit three times to double-check our math and plan. _So here’s a peek at my new office:_
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520909/513697233/-1719677217?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2024-11-22_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTI0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWFkYjk3NS0yNGE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hIjWgpe-8r4xd9t9Dw8YAgzSOg7kSq8gc50s56BZpM8=)
Come January 3rd, that doormat will read _**“Welcome to Maryland,”**_ and this office will be the property of the people of Maryland’s 3rd Congressional District!

Sometimes, it’s all too easy to get caught up in the noise of the world, but I’m committed to making this a space for collaboration and progress. [_**It’s time to get to work. Will you contribute today to help us build a strong foundation for 2025?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520910/513697234/-1719677217?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2024-11-22_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTI0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWFkYjk3NS0yNGE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hIjWgpe-8r4xd9t9Dw8YAgzSOg7kSq8gc50s56BZpM8=)
| [CONTRIBUTE ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97520911/513697235/-1719677217?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2024-11-22_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTI0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWFkYjk3NS0yNGE5LWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hIjWgpe-8r4xd9t9Dw8YAgzSOg7kSq8gc50s56BZpM8=)

Sarah Elfreth

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 11/22/2024
body:
Boebert’s ZIP code bill boosting rural towns passes House Oversight Committee with unanimous support
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Happy Friday!
Lots to update you on as we head into the weekend. Make sure you [follow us on social media](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) for important updates from now until the Sunday News Digest!
**Here in Colorado...** Lauren Boebert's [bipartisan ZIP Code bill](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), designed to boost resources for rural towns, cleared the House Oversight Committee with unanimous support. Mesa County commissioners received updates on their [pretrial and corrections services programs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), highlighting efforts to improve public safety and reduce recidivism in the community. Western Colorado school board voted to close one elementary school, with two more closures under consideration due to budget constraints and [declining enrollment](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
**Meanwhile, in Denver...** Denver Mayor Mike Johnston has vowed to [resist Trump administration deportation measures](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), likening the potential clash to “Tiananmen Square” and sparking a national conversation about sanctuary cities. Separately, Denver has come under scrutiny for spending [millions of bond dollars on schools that may soon close](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Not far from our capital city, Jefferson County Clerk Amanda Gonzalez faced backlash for spending [$4,000 of public funds on a voter outreach billboard](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) featuring her name and face.
**Not featured in today's newsletter, but for additional reading...** Matt Gaetz has officially [withdrawn his name](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) from consideration as Attorney General, with Trump announcing [Pam Bondi as his new choice](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) for U.S. Attorney General. Chris Wright and Doug Burgum are Trump’s [energy ‘dream team,'](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) focusing on [reducing energy poverty](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and advancing a diverse energy policy. Marjorie Taylor Greene has been named [Chair of the newly established DOGE Subcommittee](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) (more on DOGE [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)). And Howard Lutnick is Trump's nominee for [Secretary of Commerce](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
Don't miss today's RMV original commentary where Ellen Graham critiques the performative [activism of liberal white women](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), likening their social and political strategies to “mean girl” behavior.
This is the Mountain Minute for Friday, November 22, 2024.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Boebert’s ZIP code bill boosting rural towns passes House Oversight Committee with unanimous support](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Rural America is home to nearly 60 million individuals. That’s one out of every five Americans. Many argue that policymakers overlook rural America. Yet a simple bill led by U.S. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Silt) will have a big impact on small towns.
“I am thrilled to see my bipartisan Zip Code bill pass through the House Oversight Committee with unanimous bipartisan support,” Boebert said this week in a statement.
The introduction of ZIP codes in the 60s transformed mail delivery. They also became vital to economic growth, public safety and community identity. But many communities still face challenges without unique ZIP Codes, leading to misdirected tax revenue and delayed emergency responses.
Congresswoman Boebert took action to address this issue with [H.R. 8753](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), introduced on June 14, 2024. The bipartisan legislation originally included 31 towns across 14 states, from California to Colorado and New York.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Mesa County commissioners updated on pretrial and corrections services programs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Mesa County commissioners received an update on Pretrial Services (PTS) and Community Corrections programs, which support arrested individuals through assessments, supervision, and community-based sentencing.
* The PTS program uses the Colorado Pretrial Assessment to inform bond decisions and offers services like electronic monitoring, mental health support, and structured public service to help individuals meet court requirements.
* Summit View Treatment Services, a county initiative, provides residential and outpatient addiction treatment and mental health programs, with evidence-based therapy tailored to address trauma, substance abuse, and pro-social behavior development.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Western Colorado school board votes to close one elementary school, two more could shutter](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The Mesa County Valley District 51 school board unanimously voted to close Nisley Elementary School due to long-standing underperformance and financial pressures, with plans to integrate students into better-supported programs.
* Public input during the seven-hour meeting was emotional, with parents, teachers, and community members voicing concerns about the impact on students, community morale, and local recruitment challenges.
* Further decisions regarding the closure of Scenic and Clifton elementary schools will be made after reviewing demographic data, as the district grapples with declining enrollment and resource constraints.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[‘Like Tiananmen square’: Denver mayor vows city police, population will forcibly resist Trump deportation measures](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE DAILY CALLER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Denver Mayor Mike Johnston compared potential Trump deportation measures to the Chinese Communist Party's tactics, vowing the city’s police and residents would forcibly resist such actions.
* Johnston proposed using local law enforcement and community members to block federal immigration enforcement, emphasizing strong opposition to deportation measures.
* Johnston also stated he does not believe Gov. Jared Polis would mobilize the state National Guard for Trump’s plans, predicting federal forces might need to come from other states.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Denver has spent millions in voter-approved bond money to upgrade schools it might close](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [CHALKBEAT COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Denver Public Schools has spent $22.6 million in voter-approved bond funds over the past four years to upgrade schools now being considered for closure due to declining enrollment.
* The closures could affect 10 schools, with five standalone buildings potentially becoming vacant, prompting questions from parents and teachers about the use of bond money on schools slated for shutdown.
* The district argues that the renovations, including air conditioning and accessibility upgrades, were necessary for students and future reuse, but the proposal to shutter or partially close schools has sparked significant community pushback.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Colorado county clerk spent $4,000 on get-out-the-vote billboard with her name, face on it](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Jefferson County Clerk Amanda Gonzalez spent $7,200 on a get-out-the-vote campaign featuring a $4,000 billboard prominently displaying her image and name, sparking speculation about her potential candidacy for Colorado Secretary of State in 2026.
* Gonzalez's office defended the campaign as an effort to encourage early voting and save taxpayer dollars, noting it contributed to Jefferson County completing 98.5% of ballot counting on election night.
* Critics raised concerns about the ethics of such advertisements, as past laws have prohibited election officials running for office from using public funds for self-promotional campaigns.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Graham: White liberal women are ‘The Mean Girls’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY ELLEN GRAHAM, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_Why do other women keep telling me they’ve been unfriended for voting for Trump? Why is this a thing?_
_Having been through it myself, my pulse quickens and suddenly I’m in junior high and there they are. The mean girls. Today they are white, liberal, women, living in self-reflecting vacuums and their entitlement runs deep. They are virtuous and experts on everything. Ruthless, alone, predatory in groups, these mean girls stalk the hallways of their modern neighborhoods, children’s schools and Whole Foods looking for those who aren’t worthy._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**

With the appointment of Chris Wright as Secretary of Energy, our state once again finds itself in a key position of influence in Washington, D.C._

Maybe it was a church, where the pastor was not living the message, or a volunteer organization, where the leaders were taking advantage of their positions._
